title: Getting Started with groots
text: Registry: Defines allowable kinds nodes graph. Graph: main immutable data structure carrying nodes, edges, gates. Operations return new graph bump internal $version. Nodes & Edges: Represent units computation (data) dependencies. Gates: Explicit structural blockers attached edges, prevent downstream nodes becoming ready gate resolved. Planning: Topologically sorting tracking structural readiness nodes.

title: Getting Started with groots
text: First, establish registry strictly define kind nodes allowed graphs.
code: library(groots)  reg <- groots_registry(   groots_kind("input", output_type = "data.frame"),   groots_kind("model"),   groots_kind("report") )

title: Getting Started with groots
text: Start empty graph, add nodes edges. groots uses value semantics, every mutating function returns new updated graph instance.
code: g <- groots_graph(reg)  g <- g |>   groots_add_node(id = "data", kind = "input", label = "Raw Data") |>   groots_add_node(id = "fit", kind = "model", label = "Statistical Fit") |>   groots_add_node(id = "plot", kind = "report", label = "Final Plot") |>   groots_add_edge(from = "data", to = "fit", id = "e_data_fit") |>   groots_add_edge(from = "fit", to = "plot", id = "e_fit_plot")  # The internal version tracks changes g$version #> [1] 5

title: Getting Started with groots
text: can place “gate” edge represent manual external block—instance, requiring human approval statistical fit proceeds.

title: Getting Started with groots
text: Use groots_recompute_state() evaluate graph topologically determine nodes structurally ready blocked. can generate structural plan see dependencies shake :
code: g_state <- groots_recompute_state(g)  # The 'data' node is a root and has no blockers groots_eligible(g_state) #> [1] "data"  # The 'fit' node is blocked by the gate; the 'plot' node is blocked because 'fit' is blocked. groots_blocked(g_state) #> $fit #> [1] "gate" #>  #> $plot #> [1] "upstream_blocked" plan <- groots_plan(g_state) plan$targets #> [1] "data" "fit"  "plot" plan$blocked #> $fit #> [1] "gate" #>  #> $plot #> [1] "upstream_blocked" plan$pending_gates #> [1] "approval_gate"

title: Getting Started with groots
text: external condition met, can resolve gate. Computing state reveals downstream dependencies now unblocked.
code: g_unblocked <- groots_resolve_gate(g_state, id = "approval_gate") |>   groots_recompute_state()  # Everything is unblocked and ready for execution groots_eligible(g_unblocked) #> [1] "data" "fit"  "plot" groots_blocked(g_unblocked) #> named list()

title: Getting Started with groots
text: groots includes standard helpers topological traversal queries.

title: Tools for groots
text: groots pure, value-oriented graph library R. designed manage graph structures, topological dependencies, structural validity, planning, specifically without coupling external state runtime execution engines.

title: Tools for groots
text: groots provides purely functional API build graphs, establish dependencies, track structural blockers “gates”:
code: library(groots)  # 1. Define the kinds of nodes allowed in your graph registry <- groots_registry(   groots_kind("data_source"),   groots_kind("transform") )  # 2. Create an empty graph graph <- groots_graph(registry)  # 3. Add nodes and edges graph <- graph |>   groots_add_node("raw_data", "data_source") |>   groots_add_node("cleaned_data", "transform") |>   groots_add_edge(from = "raw_data", to = "cleaned_data", id = "edge_1")  # 4. Add a structural blocker (gate) to an edge graph <- groots_add_gate(graph, edge_id = "edge_1", id = "review_gate")  # 5. Compute the structural state graph <- groots_recompute_state(graph)  # See which nodes are eligible to proceed groots_eligible(graph) ## [1] "raw_data" # See which nodes are blocked, and why groots_blocked(graph) ## $cleaned_data ## [1] "gate" # 6. Resolve the gate to unblock the downstream node graph <- groots_resolve_gate(graph, "review_gate") |>   groots_recompute_state()  groots_eligible(graph) ## [1] "raw_data"     "cleaned_data"

title: groots 0.1.0
text: Constructors: Functions create define kinds, registries, core graph structure (groots_kind(), groots_registry(), groots_graph()). Graph Editing: Implemented structural modifiers add/remove/update nodes, edges, explicit blockers called gates. Topology Queries: Topological sorting traversal utilities (ancestors, descendants, upstream, downstream, roots, leaves, reachability). Structural Planning: Determine node eligibility blocked reasons declarative resolution without side-effects executing runtime jobs (groots_recompute_state(), groots_plan()).

title: groots 0.1.0
text: structures fully align plain-data spec serialized purely nested named lists (JSON compatible). Defined explicit typed errors abort_groots(). Removed hello() template code.
